title: lithocardium obovatum
intro: Lithocardium obovatum is a plant-based medication, also known as digoxin, which is derived from the foxglove plant (Digitalis purpurea). It is a cardiac glycoside used to treat conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure by increasing the strength of heart muscle contractions and slowing the heart rate. The medicinal properties of lithocardium obovatum include its ability to regulate heart rhythms and improve symptoms of heart failure, and its health benefits include reducing the risk of hospitalization and improving quality of life for patients with heart conditions. Lithocardium obovatum is typically prepared as a tablet or capsule for oral administration, and can also be given intravenously in some cases.
